tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: The media calibration of Self-Adhesive Vinyl (SAV) takes longer

abstract.p: The media calibration of Self-Adhesive Vinyl (SAV) takes longer.

fig.title: Difference between calibration on paper and SAV

abstract.p: Some media, like self-adhesive have a small micro bulge after being transported forward. This negatively impacts the print quality because the media is not stationary during printing.

In order to improve the print quality, media back-stepping has been added. This means that the media step will become a bit larger and then a small step back is performed, to improve the tension in the media.
The optimal size of the back-step is media dependent, so the media handling calibration has been extended with an Advanced Media Step Calibration. This calibration takes an additional 3.5 minutes.
By default, the Advanced Media Step Calibration is enabled for SAV media. For the remaining media, the calibration is by default disabled.
The Advanced Media Step Calibration can be enabled or disabled as media setting in the RIP.
